text:
Many researchers predict that artificial intelligence (AI) will continue to advance rapidly. They discuss two main types: general AI, which can perform many tasks, and artificial general intelligence (AGI), which matches human ability across all areas. Some experts believe AGI could arrive within the next twenty to fifty years.

The potential benefits are enormous. AI could revolutionise healthcare, education, and scientific discovery, solving problems too complex for humans alone. However, there are also serious risks, such as job displacement, privacy concerns, and the challenge of controlling superintelligent systems.

Timeline predictions vary widely. Optimists expect AGI by 2040, while sceptics think it may take centuries. Ultimately, how this technology develops depends on careful research and global cooperation.
